title: Water Kefir Preparation (5 gallon batch)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"agbu2","text":"Instructions for the preparation of a 5 gallon batch of 10% DBS medium to be used for water kefir. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Using the 4,000 mL cylinder to measure out 18,900 L of filtered water and add it to the stock pot.
Turn on the hot plate and turn the temperature all the way up. Add the stir bar to the stock pot and begin heating the water.
Weigh out 1,890 grams of dark brown sugar and add it to the pot.

Turn on the magnetic stirrer on the hot plate to mix the sugar. The stir bar often doesn't move and you'll need to use the ladle to stir the sugar so it doesn't stick to the bottom of the pot, and to make it easier for the stir bar to move.
Cover the pot with the lid to speed up the boiling process. (Placing paper towel under the edges of the lid helps with the noise of the lid rattling)
Bring the 10% DBS solution to a full boil for several minutes to ensure bacteria is killed. It takes about 1.5-2 hours to reach this point.
After boiling, remove the pot from the hot plate and turn it off. Place the stock pot on the blue mat on the floor, leaving it covered.
Unplug the scale and hot plate, sweep up sugar and mop up any sticky spots on the floor.
If you noticed a strong kefir smell in the room, it's time to mop, as the smell attracts fruit flies. (just use hot water and a heavy pour of bleach).
creator: Nicole Hinkley-Kellum
